title: iris aphylla subsp. fieberi
intro: Iris aphylla subsp. fieberi, also known as the Fieber's iris, is a subspecies of the Iris aphylla plant, a type of flowering plant in the Iris family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The Fieber's iris is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and it has been used to treat conditions like skin irritations and infections. Herbal preparations of Iris aphylla subsp. fieberi can be found in some traditional medicine products, often in the form of infusions, tinctures, or creams, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region and local regulations.
